sanskrit_devanagari:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते ।
लभते च तत: कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ॥ २२ ॥
sanskrit_iast: sa tayā śraddhayā yuktastasyārādhanam īhatelabhate ca tataḥ kāmānmayaiva vihitān hi tān

translations.verbatim_quote: Endowed with such a faith, he endeavors to worship a particular demigod and obtains his desires. But in actuality these benefits are bestowed by Me alone.
Endowed with such a faith, he endeavors to worship a particular demigod and obtains his desires. But in actuality these benefits are bestowed by Me alone.

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt_status: captured (fair-use excerpt; full purport length: 1540 chars)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The demigods cannot award benedictions to their devotees without the permission of the Supreme Lord. The living entity may forget that everything is the property of the Supreme Lord, but the demigods do not forget.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.22 Yuktah,being endued; taya, with that; sraddhaya, faith, as granted by Me; sah, that person; ihate, engages in; radhanam, i.e. aradhanam, worshipping; tasyah, that form of the deity.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-22-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Adi Shankaracharya of Advaita Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Endued with that faith granted by Krishna (7.21), he engages in the worship of that very form (tasyāḥ — feminine, of that deity) and obtains the desired fruits from there. Those fruits are dispensed by Krishna alone, the omniscient supreme God who apportions results.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 7.22 He, endowed with that faith without obstacles, performs the worship of Indra and other divinities. Thence, i.e., from the worship of Indra and other divinities, who constitute My body, he attains the objects of his desire, which are in reality granted by Me alone.
commentaries.raw_full_path: data/sources/raw/bg-7-22-bgus.json (key: Commentary by Sri Ramanuja of Sri Sampradaya:)
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Endowed with that obstacle-free faith, he performs the worship of Indra and other divinities, who constitute Krishna's body. From the worship of those divinities (= Krishna's body) he obtains the objects of his desire, in reality granted by Krishna alone — though the worshipper does not know at the time that the divinity is Krishna's body and that the worship is Krishna's worship.

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: All four readings agree that the granting (vihitān) is Krishna's, even when the worshipper attributes the result to the proximate deity. Shankara names Krishna as the omniscient apportioner of fruits across all worship-streams; Ramanuja names the deities themselves as Krishna's body, so worship-of-them is already worship-of-Krishna. The engineering layer respects what all readings agree on (the granting agency is upstream of the proximate worship-form) and tags STRETCHED honestly where the verse extends a divine agency-claim that the engineering analog cannot inherit.

literal_meaning: Endowed with that faith, he engages in worship of that form and obtains his desires from there — those very desires being granted by Me alone.
traditional_meaning_consensus: sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate / labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān — endowed with that faith (which Krishna has just stabilised in 7.21), the devotee performs worship of that form and obtains the desired results from that quarter; but the granting is in fact Krishna's. Shankara: the labhate is real, the dispensation is Krishna's — the worshipper sees the deity as the proximate cause, but the apportionment of fruits across all worship-streams is Krishna's, the omniscient apportioner. Ramanuja: the worshipped divinity is itself Krishna's body; the worship is in reality Krishna's worship; the result, though attributed to the divinity, is granted by Krishna alone.

remaining_gaps: Madhva, Abhinavagupta, Sridhara Swami, Kesava Kashmiri commentaries available in raw/ but not pulled into the source pack.
